title: Certification Compliance Matrix
description: Create a compliance matrix for an aircraft feature or subsystem, linking regulations to means of compliance, evidence owners, and closure status.
whyItMatters: Certification work is where engineering, regulation, and documentation converge. A strong compliance matrix keeps programmes from discovering late that nobody owns a critical evidence thread.

steps.title: Select the certification scope
steps.description: Choose a subsystem or change such as an autopilot function, fuel-system modification, or cabin safety feature.
steps.tips: A manageable scope makes the matrix deep enough to be useful.

steps.title: Extract applicable requirements
steps.description: Identify the governing regulations, advisory material, and any special conditions that apply to the scope.
steps.tips: Do not stop at one top-level regulation. Follow the evidence chain.

steps.title: Assign means of compliance
steps.description: For each requirement, decide whether compliance comes from analysis, test, inspection, similarity, or a mix.
steps.tips: The best matrix makes evidence strategy visible early.

steps.title: Map evidence ownership
steps.description: Name the responsible team, expected artefact, and current maturity for each compliance item.
steps.tips: Ownership gaps are often the most important output of the exercise.

steps.title: Review for programme risk
steps.description: Highlight the items most likely to slip, require authority negotiation, or trigger additional testing.
steps.tips: This is where the matrix becomes a management tool, not just a spreadsheet.

deliverables: Risk summary of the most vulnerable evidence items

rubric.description: Applicable requirements are identified cleanly and interpreted sensibly.
Every requirement maps to evidence and an owner.
The compliance approach is realistic for a real programme.
The matrix surfaces the most consequential open issues.
